subject: Cumulative and Survival Distribution Functions
question: A random variable $X$ has cumulative distribution function $F(x) = \begin{cases} 0 & x\le 0 \\ \frac{x^2}{4} & 0\le x < 1 \\ \frac{1+x}{4} & 1\le x < 2 \\ 1 & x\ge 2 \end{cases}$ (a) What is the probability that $X = 0$? What is the probability that $X = 1$? What is the probability that $X = 2$? (b) What is the probability that $\frac{1}{2} < X\le 1$? (c) What is the probability that $\frac{1}{2}\le X < 1$? (d) What is the probability that $X > 1.5$? Hint: You may find it helpful to sketch this function.
answer: (a) We have $\Pr(X = 0) = (\text{jump in } F(x) \text{ at } x = 0) = 0$, $\Pr(X = 1) = (\text{jump in } F(x) \text{ at } x = 1) = \frac{1}{2} - \frac{1}{4} = \frac{1}{4}$, $\Pr(X = 2) = (\text{jump in } F(x) \text{ at } x = 2) = 1 - \frac{3}{4} = \frac{1}{4}$ (b) $\frac{7}{16}$ (c) $\frac{3}{16}$ (d) $\frac{1}{4}$
explanation: Answer: (a) We have $\Pr(X = 0) = (\text{jump in } F(x) \text{ at } x = 0) = 0$, $\Pr(X = 1) = (\text{jump in } F(x) \text{ at } x = 1) = \frac{1}{2} - \frac{1}{4} = \frac{1}{4}$, $\Pr(X = 2) = (\text{jump in } F(x) \text{ at } x = 2) = 1 - \frac{3}{4} = \frac{1}{4}$ (b) $\frac{7}{16}$ (c) $\frac{3}{16}$ (d) $\frac{1}{4}$
